Package org.apache.juneau.commons.inject

Class JsrSupport

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.JsrSupport
public final class JsrSupport extends Object
FQN-based support for JSR-style annotation detection.

Centralizes all framework annotation-name matching for:

  • JSR-330 style injection annotations (jakarta.inject / javax.inject).
  • JSR-250 lifecycle annotations (jakarta.annotation / javax.annotation).
  • Spring's Autowired annotation.

Juneau intentionally matches by annotation class name so modules can interoperate with these annotations without compile-time dependencies on their APIs.

  • Method Details

    • isInjectAnnotation

      public static boolean isInjectAnnotation(AnnotationInfo<?> annotation)
      Returns true if the annotation is an inject marker.

    • isNamedAnnotation

      public static boolean isNamedAnnotation(AnnotationInfo<?> annotation)
      Returns true if the annotation is a named qualifier.

    • isQualifierMeta

      public static boolean isQualifierMeta(AnnotationInfo<?> annotation)
      Returns true if this annotation type itself is annotated with a qualifier marker.

    • isSingletonAnnotation

      public static boolean isSingletonAnnotation(AnnotationInfo<?> annotation)
      Returns true if the annotation is a singleton marker.

    • isProviderType

      public static boolean isProviderType(Class<?> type)
      Returns true if the class is a supported provider interface.

    • isPostConstructAnnotation

      public static boolean isPostConstructAnnotation(AnnotationInfo<?> annotation)
      Returns true if the annotation is a post-construct lifecycle marker.

    • isPreDestroyAnnotation

      public static boolean isPreDestroyAnnotation(AnnotationInfo<?> annotation)
      Returns true if the annotation is a pre-destroy lifecycle marker.

    • qualifierValue

      public static String qualifierValue(AnnotationInfo<?> annotation)
      Reads qualifier value from an annotation when supported.

      For @Named, returns the annotation value directly. For qualifier-meta annotations, this returns the annotation's own value() attribute if present and string-typed.

    • isValueAnnotation

      public static boolean isValueAnnotation(AnnotationInfo<?> annotation)
      Returns true if the annotation is a config-value marker.

      Recognizes both Juneau's @Value and Spring's org.springframework.beans.factory.annotation.Value by FQN — no compile-time Spring dependency in juneau-commons.

      Parameters:
      annotation - The annotation to inspect.
      Returns:
      true if annotation is one of the recognized @Value variants.

    • valueExpression

      public static String valueExpression(AnnotationInfo<?> annotation)
      Returns the configuration expression carried by a @Value annotation, or null if the annotation is not a recognized @Value.

      Both Juneau's @Value and Spring's @Value expose a single String value() attribute, so a uniform getValue() lookup against the annotation works for either.

      Parameters:
      annotation - The annotation to inspect.
      Returns:
      The value() string, or null if annotation is not a @Value.

    • isValidAnnotation

      public static boolean isValidAnnotation(AnnotationInfo<?> annotation)
      Returns true if the annotation is a Jakarta Bean Validation opt-in marker.

      Recognizes Jakarta 3.x (jakarta.validation.Valid), the older Javax 2.x equivalent (javax.validation.Valid), and Spring's @Validated group-selector by FQN — no compile-time Jakarta Validation or Spring dependency in juneau-commons.

      Parameters:
      annotation - The annotation to inspect.
      Returns:
      true if annotation is one of the recognized validation opt-in markers.
